formula: Subject + suggest/recommend/insist/demand + that + Subject + [BASE VERB, no -s, no tense change]
formula_visual: [VERB OF PERSUASION/DEMAND] + that + [SUBJECT] + [BASE VERB — same for he/she/it too]
when_to_use: Use the Subjunctive after verbs of suggestion, demand, insistence, or recommendation (suggest, recommend, insist, demand, propose, require) — the verb in the 'that' clause stays in its BASE form regardless of subject or tense, which is grammatically unusual and easy to 'correct' by mistake.
when_to_use_ar: استخدم صيغة الحث بعد أفعال الاقتراح أو الطلب أو الإصرار (suggest, recommend, insist, demand) — الفعل في جملة that بيفضل في صورته الأساسية بدون تغيير حسب الفاعل أو الزمن.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: This is one of the few places English deliberately breaks its own normal subject-verb agreement rule (he/she/it + verb-s), and advanced learners — who've spent years correctly adding -s for third person — often 'auto-correct' the subjunctive back to normal agreement, since it looks like an error by every other rule they know.

examples.en: I suggest that he go to the doctor.
examples.ar: أقترح إنه يروح الدكتور (لاحظ: go مش goes)

examples.en: The committee recommends that the policy be reviewed.
examples.ar: اللجنة توصي بمراجعة السياسة (be مش is)

examples.en: She insisted that he apologize immediately.
examples.ar: هي أصرت إنه يعتذر فورًا (apologize مش apologized، حتى لو الجملة الرئيسية ماضي)
examples.structure: [She insisted] + [that] + [he] + [apologize] (base form, even though 'insisted' is past tense)

examples.en: It is essential that everyone arrive on time.
examples.ar: من الضروري إن كل الناس توصل في الوقت (arrive مش arrives)

examples.en: They demanded that the report be submitted by Friday.

common_errors.wrong: I suggest that he goes to the doctor.
common_errors.correct: I suggest that he go to the doctor.
common_errors.explanation: This is the classic subjunctive error — even with 'he' (which normally takes -s), the subjunctive verb stays in its BASE form with no -s.

common_errors.wrong: She insisted that he apologized immediately.
common_errors.correct: She insisted that he apologize immediately.
common_errors.explanation: Even though 'insisted' is past tense, the subjunctive clause does NOT backshift like Reported Speech — it stays in the base form regardless of the main verb's tense.

common_errors.wrong: It is essential that everyone arrives on time.
common_errors.correct: It is essential that everyone arrive on time.
common_errors.explanation: 'It is essential/important/necessary that...' triggers the same subjunctive base-form rule as suggest/recommend/insist/demand.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: I recommend that she ___ (study) harder for the exam.
The doctor insists that the patient ___ (rest) for a week.
It is important that he ___ (be) on time tomorrow.
They demanded that the workers ___ (receive) fair pay.
The manager suggested that we ___ (not/be) late again.

quick_rule: After suggest/recommend/insist/demand/propose/require, or 'It is essential/important/vital that...', the verb in the 'that' clause is ALWAYS the base form — no -s, no tense change, no exceptions, regardless of subject or the main verb's tense.
quick_rule_ar: بعد suggest/recommend/insist/demand، الفعل في جملة that دايمًا في صورته الأساسية — بدون -s وبدون تغيير زمن.
mnemonic: SUBJUNCTIVE = a special 'SUB-rule' that quietly ignores the usual SUBJECT-verb agreement rule you know so well.
connect_to_speaking: Practice with your buddy: give 3 pieces of strong advice using 'I suggest/recommend/insist that...' — consciously resist the urge to add -s, since your instinct after years of English will fight you on this.
